summary: Kenneth Suna discusses retirement investing scenarios for different age groups, emphasizing the power of compound returns over time. While he mentions covered call ETFs (JEQQ) as one income-generating option, the video is primarily focused on long-term retirement planning philosophy rather than active options trading strategies.
takeaways: S&P 500 historical average return: 8-10% annually (after inflation adjustment to 8%)
Investor aged 50 with $0 saved needs $6,500/month for 10 years to reach $1.1M by age 60
Investor aged 20 needs only $350/month for 40 years to reach $1.1M by age 60
REIT dividend example: $0.26/month per share; need 3,200 shares ($165k) to generate $10k annually
SCD ETF dividend: $0.74/quarter per share; need 3,700 shares ($285k) to generate $10k annually
JEQQ covered call ETF: $0.34/month per share; need 2,000 shares (~$166k) to generate $11k annually
Recommended annual retirement spending: $30-50k depending on lifestyle
title: Investing at 50?! Hard but not Impossible. You NEED to Watch if You’re 20! #kennethsuna #investing
